body.$value: Which 2-Step Verification method should a technology director DISCOURAGE because it is carrier-dependent and can be intercepted, while steering users toward the strongest phishing-resistant options?
Security keys, because they require special hardware
SMS/text codes, while promoting security keys and passkeys as the strongest options
Passkeys, because they are too new to trust
The Google Authenticator app, because codes rotate too quickly
True or False: The "Only security key" 2-Step Verification policy now accepts both physical security keys AND passkeys.
True
False
What is the safest way to roll out 2SV enforcement so you do not lock users out of their accounts?
Turn enforcement on for the whole domain immediately so no one can delay enrolling
Let users enroll first using a configuration group with enforcement off (or the new-user enrollment grace period), track enrollment, then enforce, remembering that group settings override OU settings
Enforce at the top-level OU and rely on users to call the help desk when they are locked out
Disable all recovery options first, then enforce 2SV the same day
Google now mandates 2-Step Verification on admin accounts for Workspace for Education, and admins cannot bypass it. What is the only alternative to enrolling a super admin in 2SV?
Submit a support ticket to request a permanent exemption
Move the admin into a child OU where the policy is turned off
Remove the admin role from the account; otherwise the account must use 2SV, so enroll all super admins now
Set an app password so the admin can keep signing in without 2SV
Which statement about the "skip passwords" (passwordless passkey) setting is correct?
It is ON by default and immediately deletes every user's password
It is OFF by default, and enabling it does not remove the user's password fallback
It is OFF by default, but enabling it permanently removes the user's password
It can only be enabled for student accounts, not staff
Before enrolling high-risk staff in the Advanced Protection Program, what must a director do, and why?
Nothing special, because Advanced Protection makes account recovery faster and easier
Disable 2SV first, because Advanced Protection replaces it entirely

Pre-stage a backup factor (an extra security key or passkey, or recovery email plus phone), because Advanced Protection intentionally hardens recovery and losing all factors triggers a multi-day identity-verification delay
Store each user's password in a shared spreadsheet so the help desk can restore access instantly
